rationale.risk-routing: CLI classifier thresholds actions into silent, post-run, or live-review tiers.
rationale.hitl-workflow: Action guards and plan checkpoints let developers approve, deny, or revoke.
rationale.oversight-explanation: Check-ins explain triggers; summaries distinguish policy-initiated from model-initiated interruptions.
rationale.agent-scope-drift: Spec contracts, phase restrictions, and deviation check-ins bound off-mandate actions.
rationale.survey-input: Survey reports engineers' oversight preferences, concerns, and desired check-in timing.
rationale.framework: Paper presents a reusable, integratable dynamic-autonomy governance architecture.
rationale.built-system: Working Python CLI prototype is implemented, released, and benchmarked.
rationale.agentic: Agent plans and executes multi-file tasks behind artifact-level approval gates.
rationale.steering: Retrieved behavioral guidance shapes future prompts, separate from policy enforcement.
rationale.risk-security: Security sensitivity is surveyed, scored, and used to preserve mandatory scrutiny.
rationale.risk-quality: Survey quantifies subtle-bug concerns; routing and verification mitigate quality failures.
rationale.risk-overreliance: Survey measures understanding erosion; system calibrates oversight against evolving trust.
rationale.method-self-report: Professional developers report concerns and oversight preferences through a formative survey.
rationale.method-experiment: Controlled persona conditions compare Hedwig against third-party Claude Code behavior.
